steps.title: Find the first slope
steps.content:
Rewrite the given line in slope-intercept form:
$$x-10y=15$$
$$-10y=15-x$$
$$y=\frac{1}{10}x-\frac{3}{2}$$
Its slope is $\frac{1}{10}$.

steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the lines are parallel and different. The second line must therefore have slope $\frac{1}{10}$ but a different intercept.

steps.title: Check the parallel line
steps.content:
For $\frac{1}{2}x-5y=0$, multiply by $2$:
$$x-10y=0$$
This has the same slope as $x-10y=15$, but it is a different line. The answer is A.
